description_long: This is a large, roughly spherical stone barrier with a significant wedge-shaped section cut out, creating a flat, angular surface on one side. The stone is predominantly gray with a weathered, slightly mottled texture, and features blue graffiti text on one side, adding an urban, vandalized character. The carved section appears to be intentionally shaped, possibly for aesthetic or functional reasons, and the rest of the surface is rounded and irregular. The stone shows signs of exposure to the elements, with some discoloration and surface roughness. Its substantial mass and size suggest it is used as a physical obstacle or boundary marker in public spaces. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a touch of urban art due to the graffiti.
description: A large, gray, weathered stone barrier with a wedge-shaped cut and blue graffiti, showing signs of urban use and exposure.
description_view_0: The flat, angular cut and carved surface are prominent, showing the geometric detail and rough stone texture.
description_view_1: The rounded, unmarked stone surface is visible, with subtle weathering and no graffiti in this view.
description_view_2: The blue graffiti text is clearly visible, covering much of the curved stone surface and adding urban character.
description_view_3: The carved section is visible from a different angle, with part of the blue graffiti wrapping around the side.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature, often at the edge of pedestrian zones or near vehicle access points
attribute_pattern: Features a geometric carved section and blue graffiti text; the rest of the surface is mottled and irregular with no regular natural pattern.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and weathered, with visible discoloration and minor erosion, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces and urban infrastructure, often standing out due to graffiti and its sculpted form.
